LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANT RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. EVEN IF THE COURT ASSUMES AN ENCROACHMENT EXISTS, DEFENDANT APPEARS INNOCENT. DEFENDANT REASONABLY RELIED UPON THE MARCH 2007 SURVEY, AND THE APRIL 2009 MEETING APPEARING TO RESOLVE ANY BOUNDARY DISPUTE. GIVEN THE SMALL NATURE OF THE ENCROACHMENT, THE HARM TO PLAINTIFF IS MINOR. THE HARDSHIP TO DEFENDANT IS GREAT. DEFENDANT MAY STILL WORK ON THE WALL IF IT CAN DO SO WITHOUT TRESPASSING ON PLAINTIFF'S LAND. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF DENIED, DEFENDANT TO MAKE FORMAL APPLICATION FOR SUCH RELIEF. REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE DENIED. (BOTH PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE FILED, AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT HEARING.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DISCOVERY, 302 DEFENDANTS EDMOND KAVOUNAS AND MARGARET KAVOUNAS RE-NOTICE OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH COURT ORDER AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE ACCESS BY 10/15/10 AND REMIT $1,500 IN SANCTIONS TO THE COURT. THE COURT ORDERED PLAINTIFF TO ALLOW WEATHERPROOFING. PLAINTIFF'S ATTEMPT TO INJECT CONDITIONS IN THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS THAT WERE UNRELATED TO THE WEATHERPROOFING AND SUBSEQUENT REFUSAL TO PROVIDE ACCESS WAS A VIOLATION OF THIS COURT'S ORDER TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's request for OSC re: Preliminary Injunction is denied.
